issues.body: out_of_circuit_message accepts an arbitrary holders slice independently of the sources whose bytes were reconstructed. Nothing checks that holders[i] equals sources[i].holder, or even that the counts match. Thus changing a SourceSegment.holder and reconstructing the same holder-anonymous bytes while passing the old holders slice produces the identical digest. The new test does not exercise the real composition boundary: it manually changes both the holder list and digest input, so it only proves that hashing different bytes differs. This leaves the prior holder-attribution concern unresolved despite claims that relabelling or swapping a holder changes the signed digest.
issues.fix_hint: Make the binding operation consume the ordered SourceSegment list (or a validated reconstruction object carrying both bytes and holder identities) and derive holder_block internally, so identity/order cannot diverge from the reconstructed segments. Alternatively provide one public atomic function that reconstructs and hashes after validating exact count and positional identity, and keep unsafe pieces private. Add a negative test that changes only SourceSegment.holder while attempting to reuse the prior holder metadata/message; the public API must reject it or change the digest.
issues.title: Holder-to-segment binding is still caller-asserted rather than enforced

issues.body: The skill documents out_of_circuit_message(federated_public_inputs), but the public method now requires holders as its first argument. The recipe therefore does not compile and omits the security-critical holder-order input introduced to address round 1.
issues.fix_hint: Document the exact current signature, including holders, and explain that its ordering must correspond positionally to the reconstructed sources; if the API is made atomic as recommended, document that safer signature instead.
issues.title: Documented FreshnessBinding method signature is stale

verdict.summary: Round 2 honestly narrows the cross-crate test claim and adds holder IDs to the digest, so it addresses the prior drift-test overclaim and moves toward holder binding. However, the security boundary remains unenforced: the signed holder list is supplied independently from the SourceSegment holders used to construct the public inputs. A caller can relabel a SourceSegment while retaining the old holders argument and obtain the same signed message, so the API does not itself bind the holder-to-segment assignment it claims to bind. The updated public API documentation also shows the obsolete one-argument method signature. Because one prior major issue remains in a changed form and a new documentation/API issue appears, progress is regressing under the required scale.
